hub_subtitle: Business English for global coffee trading

lesson.menu_desc: Writing a clear business email -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 -- Structure, Greetings and Closings in Business Email
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Fernanda Gabriela Silva Rodrigues | Aula 5 -- First Professional Email

listenings.text: Hello, this is a short note about business emails. A good email has four parts. First, a clear subject line. Then a greeting, like Dear Maria. After that, the body with your message. Finally, a closing, like Best regards, and your signature. Keep it short and clear. If you have a file, attach it and say please find attached. Thank you.

Hi, this is Fernanda. I am writing a short message about my first email to a supplier. I start with the subject line. Then I write Dear Carlos as the greeting. In the body, I say I am writing to schedule a call about a coffee shipment. I close with Best regards and my signature. I also attach the contract. Please reply when you can. Thank you.

extra_audio.text: Fernanda opens a new email. She writes a clear subject line. She adds a greeting: Dear Carlos. In the body, she writes her message. She closes with Best regards and her signature.
